title: Intrusion Detection & Prevention Systems
description: Systems that monitor network traffic for malicious activity and can block attacks.
fullExplanation: IDS (Intrusion Detection System) monitors network traffic and alerts on suspicious activity. IPS (Intrusion Prevention System) goes further — it can actively block attacks. Both use signature-based detection (known attack patterns) and anomaly-based detection (deviations from normal behavior). IDS is passive (monitor only), IPS is inline (can drop packets).

types.name: Network IDS (NIDS)
types.description: Monitors all traffic on network segment. Placed on network tap or SPAN port.

types.name: Network IPS (NIPS)
types.description: Inline device that can block malicious traffic. All traffic flows through it.

types.name: Host IDS (HIDS)
types.description: Runs on individual hosts, monitors system calls, logs, file changes.

types.name: Host IPS (HIPS)
types.description: Runs on hosts, can block malicious processes or network connections.

detectionMethods.name: Signature-Based
detectionMethods.description: Matches traffic against database of known attack patterns

detectionMethods.name: Anomaly-Based
detectionMethods.description: Learns normal behavior, alerts on deviations

keyFacts: IDS alerts, IPS blocks — IPS is active defense
Anomaly detection has high false positives initially
IPS can cause network latency (inline processing)
Snort is the most popular open-source IDS/IPS
SIEM systems aggregate IDS/IPS alerts for analysis
